mechanism_narrative: Rising consumer price index values increase inflation expectations, prompting equity investors to demand higher risk premiums and reducing the present value of future corporate earnings, which drives negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Rank IC exceeds 0.05 or positive forward returns occur in >65% of high-inflation months over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.4 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions and reduced consumer spending capacity, triggering immediate negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Forward returns are positive in >55% of triggered instances or the signal loses statistical significance (p > 0.10) in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts indicates robust aggregate demand growth, which boosts corporate revenue expectations and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: Joint positive signals yield negative forward returns in >60% of cases or interaction term coefficient becomes statistically insignificant in multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: Declining industrial production during a monetary tightening cycle reflects constrained output capacity and weakening economic momentum, which forces equity valuations lower and produces negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Signal direction reverses to positive during tightening periods or predictive accuracy drops below 50% in regime-filtered backtests.

mechanism_narrative: Steady growth in total nonfarm payrolls reflects expanding economic activity and rising corporate profit margins, which consistently supports positive S&P 500 forward returns over short horizons.
failure_condition: Rank IC falls below -0.02 over consecutive 12-month windows or positive returns occur in <45% of high-growth payroll months.

mechanism_narrative: A federal funds rate level exceeding 2.0 percent establishes a restrictive financing environment that increases corporate borrowing costs and compresses equity multiples, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in >60% of observations above the threshold or the rate variable shows zero predictive power in quantile regression.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between rising real GDP and falling real personal consumption expenditures indicates weak household demand despite macroeconomic growth, which suppresses equity valuations and generates negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: The divergence pattern correlates with positive forward returns or the interaction effect vanishes when controlling for monetary policy shocks.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production during a post-shock recovery phase signals rapidly expanding manufacturing capacity and improving supply chain efficiency, which triggers immediate positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Signal fails to predict positive returns in >55% of recovery-phase observations or loses significance when filtered by recession exit dates.
